# **CodeCoverage per PR in Fleetcard-backend**

## Summary

The core problem is low code coverage in the codebase leads to:

* **Risk of Undetected Failures:** Lack of test coverage risks unexpected changes impacting other parts of the codebase unnoticed. Code without full coverage can break from unrelated changes, often with issues only detected in production.  
* **QA Reliance:** The team currently relies heavily on the QA team to identify and mitigate potential bugs. Critical gaps in coverage have been discovered during QA testing or, in some cases, only after changes were deployed to production.

## Impact: 

A single PR might only move global coverage by a fraction of a percent, but Over time, as developers touch more code and cover their changes, global coverage rises naturally.

## High-Level Requirements

* Run code coverage on CI.  
* Fail CI build if the code coverage of modified lines is \< 80%

## Available Approaches:

1. #### **Full Codebase-Coverage** \- Store the current code coverage score for codebase. Compare and check if it's depriving

   Drawbacks:   
- Ex: consider scenario when there were 4 / 8 lines covered. But now 5 / 10 lines covered. Its still 50% but only 1 of the 2 new lines got covered.  
-  We need to store the current coverage for each file somewhere but this could change after every commit.

2. #### **Diff Coverage** (Proposed)

   Principle: **coverage% for only the lines modified in the pr**  
   diff-coverage%= \#lines modified in the pr with tests / total modified lines in pr  
   get line by line changes from **git diff** & check if the lines of code are covered in tests & optionally fail ci and print the reason for failure in ci log. Optionally add a PR comment/slack ping with result from coverage report

## Proposed Solution (Diff coverage):

3 step ci job:  
![][image1]

## How It Works — Step by Step

| ``PR opened / updated         │         ▼   Checkout (fetch-depth: 0 — required for merge-base walk)         │         ▼   Install Ruby, bundle install         │         ▼   Run test-suite & generate 1 coverage map         │ 	  _________________________________________________________________________         │							OR			        │         │										        │         ▼										        │   Determine PR merge-base & Parse git diff						 ▼     ├── parse +++ / @@ headers manually						Use undercover gem or diff-cover py package     ├── Skip _spec.rb, _test.rb, db/migrate/, config/                         that do this natively     └── Produces changed_lines { file → [line numbers] }                       |         │                                                                        |         ▼                                                                        |   Intersect diff lines with coverage map                                          |     ├── For each changed file → filter to executable lines (non-nil)           |     ├── Count covered (> 0) vs total executable changed lines                   |     └── Accumulate totals across all files                                      |         │                                                                        |         ▼                                                                        |   Compute aggregate patch coverage                                                |     aggregate_pct = (total_covered / total_coverable) * 100                       |         │                                                                        | 	  _________________________________________________________________________         ▼   Enforce 80% threshold     ├── PASS → print ✅ summary, exit 0     └── FAIL → print ❌ aggregate %, per-file breakdown,                uncovered line numbers → exit 1         │         ▼   Post PR comment     ├── Always runs (even on failure — use `if: always()`)     ├── Posts markdown table: file | changed lines | covered | %     └── Uses GITHUB_TOKEN (pull-requests: write)         │         ▼   Persist coverage summary (long-term history)     ├── for coverage trend analysis or future references``  |
| :---- |

## Industry Patterns for Diff Coverage:

| Solution | Diff-Coverage(coverage% for only the lines modified in the pr) | Scoped thresholding By % (Support for multiple coverage% thresholding for some group of files/folders)  | PR Comments (commenting the calculated coverage% in the pr-comments along with storing in logs/artifacts) | PR Gating (failing/blocking the pr based on coverage% rules) | Cons |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| [undercover](https://github.com/grodowski/undercover) CLI () \+ gh-action | ✅ (line-level) (Ruby specific app) | ❌ (Only shows Hit/Miss for overall lines/blocks) | ❌Need to handle manually | ✅fail ci via gh-action | Cant give numeric “X% of changed lines are covered”. Only shows hit/miss per line |
| UndercoverCI App | ✅ | ❌(Only shows Hit/Miss per lines/blocks) | ✅  | ✅  | Same as above \+ Paid saas |
| [Codecov SaaS](https://about.codecov.io/) | ✅ | ✅ (Components, paid) | ✅ native | ✅  | Paid saas |
| Custom script \+ gh-action  | ✅(Analyse git diff manually) | ✅ (full control) | ✅ (use existing git token) | ✅ Fail ci via gh-actions | Need to store Previous Full-coverage % for full-codebase coverage trends |
| [Datadog Code-Coverage](https://docs.datadoghq.com/code_coverage/#:~:text=block%20merges%20based%20on%20configurable%20coverage%20thresholds.) | ✅Has both Full & Diff coverage | ✅ diff thresholds to diff paths (by **CODEOWNERS**, etc)  | ❌(only shows status in pr, devs need to checkout DD page for per file coverage% change) | ✅ [ref-image](?tab=t.2dhgd0nlqjbg) | Free when **Test Optimization** activated in plan |

| K2web Datadog [code-coverage dashboard](https://app.datadoghq.com/ci/code-coverage/github.com%2Fkeeptruckin%2Fk2web/branch?filterBy=codeowner&fromUser=false&selectedBranch=master&selectedCommitBranch=master&viewBy=file-explorer&start=1771683731133&end=1774275731133&paused=false) k2web is only using visualization, per pr coverage %es but NOT ci checks feature of DD |
| :---- |

## RISKS

* **Unreliability of global coverage threshold** (e.g., "the project must maintain 80% coverage") fails for the 4/8 → 5/10 case  
* **Maintaining current coverage limit for coverage trend analysis:** We need to store the current coverage for full codebase somewhere but this could change after every commit.

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
